message:
test(conformance): oracle + proptest + fixture coverage for new parity work

crates/fp-conformance/oracle/pandas_oracle.py (+74):
 - `op_series_mode`: new oracle handler mirroring pandas' Series.mode
   with dropna passthrough.
 - `op_series_nunique`: counterpart for nunique with dropna.
 - `op_series_rank`: new handler for Series.rank including method
   selection (average, min, max, first, dense) and na_option
   handling (keep/top/bottom). Raises OracleError on missing left
   payload instead of silently returning the wrong shape.

crates/fp-conformance/tests/proptest_properties.rs (+318):
 New property-test strategies and cases for the operations that
 landed in fp-frame/fp-groupby/fp-index above, covering:
  - Series/DataFrame nunique/mode with both dropna values
  - DataFrame mode axis=0/1 with numeric_only variants
  - GroupBy sort=True/False parity for every aggregate
  - Index drop_duplicates Keep::{First, Last, None}
  - Datetime/timedelta NaT counting invariants
 Strategies are seeded deterministically so failures are
 reproducible from the corpus.

crates/fp-conformance/COVERAGE.md:
 - GroupBy fixtures: 24 -> 27
 - Total fixtures: 811 -> 814
 - Groupby_sum/mean/count notes updated to reflect the new null-key
   dropna + non-null count coverage.

fixtures/packets/fp_p2d_063_dataframe_melt_auto_value_vars_strict.json:
 - Was a degenerate single-row fixture; rebuilt with a realistic
   multi-row (id = r1/r2) source so the melt->long form actually
   exercises the auto-inferred value_vars path.

fixtures/packets/ -- 35 new packet JSON fixtures spanning:
  - groupby_sum/count/mean null-key + null-skip strict fixtures
  - dataframe reset_index/set_index duplicate-label strict fixtures
  - drop_duplicates subset keep=None fixture
  - duplicated semantic-NaN equality fixture
  - mode all-missing / exact-numeric-equivalence
  - melt explicit value_vars ordering
  - crosstab normalize all/columns + get_dummies utf8 hardened
  - pivot_table margins_name + mean fill hardened
  - stack preserves column order + sparse all-null row hardened
  - abs duplicate-index + NaN passthrough
  - reindex duplicate/missing label mixes
  - idxmin nonnumeric-NaN + ties-first
  - nunique exact-numeric / signed-zero
  - quantile even-length / nonnumeric skip
  - value_counts signed-zero / tie order
  - memory_usage mixed dtypes / utf8 index

(Four FP-P2D-148/159/160 fixtures held back due to active file
reservations by another agent; will commit in a follow-up once
the reservations clear.)

+{
+  "packet_id": "FP-P2D-164",
+  "case_id": "dataframe_memory_usage_utf8_index_strict",
+  "mode": "strict",
+  "operation": "dataframe_memory_usage",
+  "frame": {
+    "index": [
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "row-0" },
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "x" },
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "" },
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a much longer label" }
+    ],
+    "column_order": ["a", "b"],
+    "columns": {
+      "a": [
+        { "kind": "float64", "value": 1.0 },
+        { "kind": "float64", "value": 2.0 },
+        { "kind": "float64", "value": 3.0 },
+        { "kind": "float64", "value": 4.0 }
+      ],
+      "b": [
+        { "kind": "bool", "value": true },
+        { "kind": "bool", "value": true },
+        { "kind": "bool", "value": false },
+        { "kind": "bool", "value": false }
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "expected_series": {
+    "index": [
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "Index" },
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "a" },
+      { "kind": "utf8", "value": "b" }
+    ],
+    "values": [
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 32 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 32 },
+      { "kind": "int64", "value": 4 }
+    ]
+  }
+}

@@ -270,6 +270,24 @@ def op_series_div(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     return op_series_binary_numeric(pd, payload, "series_div")
 
 
+def op_series_mode(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("series"), "series_mode")
+    dropna = payload.get("mode_dropna")
+    if dropna is None:
+        dropna = True
+    out = series.mode(dropna=bool(dropna))
+    return {"expected_series": series_to_expected(out)}
+
+
+def op_series_nunique(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    series = fixture_series_from_payload(pd, payload.get("series"), "series_nunique")
+    dropna = payload.get("nunique_dropna")
+    if dropna is None:
+        dropna = True
+    out = series.nunique(dropna=bool(dropna))
+    return {"expected_scalar": scalar_to_json(out)}
+
+
 def op_series_join(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     left = payload.get("left")
     right = payload.get("right")
@@ -1300,6 +1318,31 @@ def op_series_count(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
     return {"expected_scalar": scalar_to_json(out)}
 
 
+def op_series_rank(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    left = payload.get("left")
+    if left is None:
+        raise OracleError("series_rank requires left payload")
+
+    method = payload.get("rank_method") or "average"
+    na_option = payload.get("rank_na_option") or "keep"
+    ascending = payload.get("sort_ascending")
+    if ascending is None:
+        ascending = True
+    pct = bool(payload.get("rank_pct", False))
+
+    index = [label_from_json(item) for item in left["index"]]
+    values = [scalar_from_json(item) for item in left["values"]]
+    series = pd.Series(values, index=index, name=left.get("name", "series"))
+    out = series.rank(method=method, ascending=ascending, na_option=na_option, pct=pct)
+
+    return {
+        "expected_series": {
+            "index": [label_to_json(v) for v in out.index.tolist()],
+            "values": [scalar_to_json(v) for v in out.tolist()],
+        }
+    }
+
+

@@ -3020,8 +3063,22 @@ def op_dataframe_mode(pd, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:

         raise OracleError("dataframe_mode requires frame payload")

+    if axis is None:
+        axis = 0

+
+    dropna = payload.get("mode_dropna")

     frame = dataframe_from_json(pd, frame_payload)
-    out = frame.mode()

     return {"expected_frame": dataframe_to_json(out)}
